controls.control_description:
B.11 Post investment changes / additional investment (or financial
commitment) in existing JV / WOS
A JV / WOS set up by the Indian Party as per the Regulations may diversify its
activities / set up step down subsidiary / alter the shareholding pattern in the
overseas entity (subject to compliance of Regulation 7 of the Notification ibid, in the
case of financial services sector companies).

controls.control_objective: To ensure report to the reserve bank through the ad category - i bank, the details of such decisions within 30 days of the approval of those decisions by the co
controls.control_description:
The Indian Party should report to the
Reserve Bank through the AD Category - I bank, the details of such decisions
within 30 days of the approval of those decisions by the competent authority of the
JV / WOS concerned in terms of local laws of the host country and include the
<image>
same in the Annual Performance Report (APR - Part II of Form ODI) required to be
forwarded to the AD Category-I bank.

controls.control_description:
B.12 Restructuring of the balance sheet of the overseas entity involving
write off of capital and receivables
In order to provide more operational flexibility to the Indian corporates, the Indian
promoters who have set up WOS abroad or have at least 51 per cent stake in an
overseas JV, may write off capital (equity / preference shares) or other receivables,
such as, loans, royalty, technical knowhow fees and management fees in respect of
the JV /WOS, even while such JV /WOS continues to function as under:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to write off capital (equity / preference shares) or other receivables, such as, loans, royalty, technical knowhow fees and management fees in respect of

controls.control_description:
The write-
off / restructuring is subject to the condition that the Indian Party should submit the
following documents for scrutiny along with the applications to the designated AD
Category -I bank under the Automatic as well as the Approval Routes:
a) A certified copy of the balance sheet showing the loss in the overseas
WOS/JV set up by the Indian Party;

controls.control_description:
B.13 Acquisition of a foreign company through bidding or tender procedure
An Indian Party may remit earnest money deposit or issue a bid bond guarantee for
acquisition of a foreign company through bidding and tender procedure and also
make subsequent remittances through an AD Category - I bank in accordance with
the provisions of Regulation 14 of the Notification ibid.

controls.control_description:

further period as Reserve Bank may permit, from the date of effecting remittance or
the date on which the amount to be capitalised became due to the Indian Party or
the date on which the amount due was allowed to be capitalised;

controls.control_objective: To ensure be prescribed by the reserve bank from time to time
controls.control_description:
(iii) submit to the Reserve Bank, through the designated Authorised Dealer, every
year on or before December 31, an Annual Performance Report (APR) in Part II of
Form ODI in respect of each JV or WOS outside India, and other reports or
documents as may be prescribed by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be prescribed by the reserve bank from time to time

so required to be submitted, has to be based on the audited annual accounts of the
JV/WOS for the preceding year, unless specifically exempted by the Reserve Bank.

controls.control_objective: To ensure necessarily check with its nodal office to confirm that all aprs in respect of all the jv / wos of the applicant have been submitted
controls.control_description:

should necessarily check with its nodal office to confirm that all APRs in
respect of all the JV / WOS of the applicant have been submitted;

controls.control_description:
B.15 Transfer by way of sale of shares of a JV / WOS
(1) An Indian Party, without prior approval of the Reserve Bank, may transfer by
way of sale to another Indian Party which complies with the provisions of
Regulation 6 of FEMA Notification 120/RB-2004 dated July 7, 2004 as amended
from time to time, or to a person resident outside India, any share or security held
by it in a JV or WOS outside India subject to the following conditions:

controls.control_description:

An Indian Party may create charge, by way of pledge, on the shares of Joint
Venture (JV) or Wholly Owned Subsidiary (WOS) or Step Down Subsidiary (SDS)
outside India as a security in favour of an Authorized Dealer or a public financial

controls.control_description:
B.18 Rollover of guarantees
(1) It has been decided not to treat / reckon the renewal / rollover of an existing /
original guarantee, which is part of the total financial commitment of the Indian
Party in terms of Regulation 6 of the Notification ibid, as a fresh financial
commitment, provided that:

controls.control_description:
B.19 Creation of charge on domestic and foreign assets
(1) An Indian Party may create charge (by way of mortgage, pledge, hypothecation
or otherwise) on its assets [including the assets of its group company, sister
concern or associate company in India, promoter and / or director] in favour of an
overseas lender as security for availing of the fund based and/or non-fund based
facility for its Joint Venture (JV) or Wholly Owned Subsidiary (WOS) or Step Down
Subsidiary (SDS) outside India subject to the terms and conditions prescribed

controls.control_description:
(2) An Indian Party may create charge (by way of mortgage, pledge, hypothecation
or otherwise) on the assets of its overseas JV or WOS or SDS in favour of an AD
bank in India as security for availing of the fund based and/or non-fund based
facility for itself or its JV or WOS or SDS outside India subject to the terms and
conditions prescribed under Regulation 18A of the Notification and A.P.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to create charge (by way of mortgage, pledge, hypothecation or otherwise) on the assets of its overseas jv or wos or sds in favour of an ad bank in india

controls.control_objective: To ensure make overseas direct investment in the equity shares and compulsorily convertible preference shares of a joint venture (jv) or wholly owned subsidiary
controls.control_description:
B.20 Overseas Direct Investments by resident individuals
With effect from August 05, 2013, a resident individual (single or in association with
another resident individual or with an 'Indian Party' as defined in the Notification)
satisfying the criteria as per Schedule V of the Notification, may make overseas
direct investment in the equity shares and compulsorily convertible preference
shares of a Joint Venture (JV) or Wholly Owned Subsidiary (WOS) outside India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to make overseas direct investment in the equity shares and compulsorily convertible preference shares of a joint venture (jv) or wholly owned subsidiary

controls.control_objective: To ensure be within the overall limit prescribed by the reserve bank of india under the provisions of liberalised remittance scheme, as prescribed by the reserv
controls.control_description:
The limit of overseas direct investment by the resident individual shall be within the
overall limit prescribed by the Reserve Bank of India under the provisions of
Liberalised Remittance Scheme, as prescribed by the Reserve Bank from time to
time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement technical controls to configure system to be within the overall limit prescribed by the reserve bank of india under the provisions of liberalised remittance scheme, as prescribed by the reserv

controls.control_objective: To ensure enter into forward / option contracts with resident entities who wish to hedge their overseas direct investments (in equity and loan)
controls.control_description:
AD Category - I banks may enter into
forward / option contracts with resident entities who wish to hedge their overseas
direct investments (in equity and loan), subject to verification of such exposure.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to enter into forward / option contracts with resident entities who wish to hedge their overseas direct investments (in equity and loan)

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to the original maturity
controls.control_description:
(2) If a hedge becomes naked in part or full owing to shrinking of the market
value of the overseas direct investment (or financial commitment), the hedge may
continue to the original maturity.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to the original maturity

controls.control_objective: To ensure open, hold and maintain foreign currency account (fca) abroad for the purpose of making overseas direct investments subject to the following terms and
controls.control_description:

from time to time, an Indian party may open, hold and maintain Foreign Currency
Account (FCA) abroad for the purpose of making overseas direct investments
subject to the following terms and conditions:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be opened, held and maintained as per the regulation of the host country
controls.control_description:
The account shall be opened, held and maintained as per the regulation of the
host country.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be opened, held and maintained as per the regulation of the host country

controls.control_objective: To ensure be utilized only for making overseas direct investment into the joint venture / wholly owned subsidiary (jv / wos) abroad
controls.control_description:
The remittances sent to the account by the Indian party should be utilized only
for making overseas direct investment into the Joint Venture / Wholly Owned
Subsidiary (JV / WOS) abroad.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be utilized only for making overseas direct investment into the joint venture / wholly owned subsidiary (jv / wos) abroad

controls.control_objective: To ensure be repatriated to india within 30 days from the date of credit
controls.control_description:
Any amount received in the account by way of dividend and / or other
entitlements from the subsidiary shall be repatriated to India within 30 days from the
date of credit.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be repatriated to india within 30 days from the date of credit

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit the details of debits and credits in the account on yearly basis to the designated ad bank with a certificate from the statutory <image> audito
controls.control_description:
The Indian party should submit the details of debits and credits in the account
on yearly basis to the designated AD bank with a certificate from the Statutory
<image>
Auditors of the Indian party certifying that the account was maintained as per the
host country laws and the extant FEMA regulations / provisions as applicable.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit the details of debits and credits in the account on yearly basis to the designated ad bank with a certificate from the statutory <image> audito

controls.control_objective: To ensure be closed immediately or within 30 days from the date of disinvestment from jv / wos or cessation thereof
controls.control_description:
The account so opened shall be closed immediately or within 30 days from the
date of disinvestment from JV / WOS or cessation thereof.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be closed immediately or within 30 days from the date of disinvestment from jv / wos or cessation thereof

controls.control_objective: To ensure the proceeds thereof are repatriated immediately on receipt thereof and in any case not later than 90 days from the date of sale of such securities
controls.control_description:
(2) A person resident in India may transfer by way of sale the shares acquired
as stated above provided that the proceeds thereof are repatriated immediately on
receipt thereof and in any case not later than 90 days from the date of sale of such
securities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the proceeds thereof are repatriated immediately on receipt thereof and in any case not later than 90 days from the date of sale of such securities

controls.control_objective: To ensure to be obtained before acquisition of a foreign security
controls.control_description:
(4) In all other cases, not covered by general or special permission, approval of
the Reserve Bank is required to be obtained before acquisition of a foreign security.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to be obtained before acquisition of a foreign security

controls.control_description:
<image>
c) rights shares provided that the rights shares are being issued by virtue of
holding shares in accordance with the provisions of law for the time being in
force;

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow its resident employees (including working directors) to purchase foreign securities under the adr/gdr linked stock option schemes
controls.control_description:
and
e) An Indian company in the knowledge based sector may allow its resident
employees (including working directors) to purchase foreign securities
under the ADR/GDR linked stock option schemes.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to allow its resident employees (including working directors) to purchase foreign securities under the adr/gdr linked stock option schemes

controls.control_objective: To ensure be governed by sebi (employees' stock option and stock purchase scheme) guidelines, 1999 and the issue of employees stock option by an unlisted compan
controls.control_description:
The issue of employees'
stock option by a listed company shall be governed by SEBI (Employees'
Stock Option and Stock Purchase Scheme) Guidelines, 1999 and the issue
of employees stock option by an unlisted company shall be governed by the
guidelines issued by the Government of India for issue of ADR/GDR linked
stock options.

controls.control_objective: To ensure exceed the ceiling as stipulated by the reserve bank from time to time
controls.control_description:
The consideration for the purchase should not exceed the
ceiling as stipulated by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to exceed the ceiling as stipulated by the reserve bank from time to time

controls.control_objective: To ensure acquire the shares of society for worldwide interbank financial telecommunication (swift) as per the by-laws of swift
controls.control_description:
C.4 Acquiring the shares of SWIFT by a resident bank
A bank in India, being licensed by the Reserve Bank under the provisions of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, may acquire the shares of Society for Worldwide
Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) as per the by-laws of SWIFT,
provided the bank has been permitted by the Reserve Bank for admission to the
'SWIFT User's Group in India' as member.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to acquire the shares of society for worldwide interbank financial telecommunication (swift) as per the by-laws of swift

controls.control_objective: To ensure issue indian depository receipts (idrs) through a domestic depository
controls.control_description:
C.5 Issue of Indian Depository Receipts (IDRs)
Eligible companies resident outside India may issue Indian Depository Receipts
(IDRs) through a Domestic Depository.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to issue indian depository receipts (idrs) through a domestic depository

controls.control_objective: To ensure be obtained before the issuance of idrs
controls.control_description:
In case of raising of funds through issuance of IDRs by
financial/banking companies having presence in India, either through a branch or
subsidiary, the approval of the sectoral regulator(s) should be obtained before the
issuance of IDRs.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be obtained before the issuance of idrs

from time to time by SEBI in this regard:

controls.control_objective: To ensure report, on a monthly basis, the balances of foreign sovereign securities, held by them as non-cash collaterals of their clearing members to the chief
controls.control_description:
Clearing Corporations shall report, on a monthly basis, the balances of foreign
sovereign securities, held by them as non-cash collaterals of their clearing
members to the Chief General Manager, Reserve Bank of India, Foreign Exchange
Department, Foreign Investment Division, Central Office, Mumbai.

controls.control_description:
The report
should be submitted by the 10th of the following month to which it relates.

controls.control_objective: To ensure to route all its transactions relating to the investment (or financial commitment) through one branch of an ad category - i bank designated by it in t
controls.control_description:
Designated branches
An eligible Indian Party making investment (or financial commitment) in a Joint
Venture (JV) / Wholly Owned Subsidiary (WOS) outside India is required to route all
its transactions relating to the investment (or financial commitment) through one
branch of an AD Category - I bank designated by it in terms of clause (v) of sub
regulation 2 of Regulation 6 of the Notification ibid.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to route all its transactions relating to the investment (or financial commitment) through one branch of an ad category - i bank designated by it in t

controls.control_objective: To ensure be routed through the same branch of the ad category - i bank that has been designated by the indian investor for the investment (or financial commitm
controls.control_description:
All communication from the
Indian Parties, to the Reserve Bank, relating to the investment (or financial
<image>
commitment) outside India should be routed through the same branch of the AD
Category - I bank that has been designated by the Indian investor for the
investment (or financial commitment).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be routed through the same branch of the ad category - i bank that has been designated by the indian investor for the investment (or financial commitm

controls.control_objective: To ensure also forward its comments / recommendations on the request
controls.control_description:
The designated AD Category - I bank while
forwarding the request from their customers to the Reserve Bank, should also
forward its comments / recommendations on the request.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also forward its comments / recommendations on the request

controls.control_objective: To ensure designate different ad category - i banks / branches of ad category - i banks for different jv / wos outside india
controls.control_description:
However, the Indian
Party may designate different AD Category - I banks / branches of AD Category - I
banks for different JV / WOS outside India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to designate different ad category - i banks / branches of ad category - i banks for different jv / wos outside india

controls.control_objective: To ensure to maintain party-wise record in respect of each jv/ wos
controls.control_description:
For proper follow up, the AD Category -
I bank is required to maintain party-wise record in respect of each JV/ WOS.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to maintain party-wise record in respect of each jv/ wos

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow investments (or financial commitment) up to the permissible limits on receipt of application in form odi together with form a-2, duly filled in
controls.control_description:
August 22, 2022
AD Category - I banks may allow investments (or financial commitment) up to the
permissible limits on receipt of application in form ODI together with form A-2, duly
filled in, from the Indian Party (ies)/ Resident Individual (RI) making investments (or
financial commitment) in a JV/WOS abroad subject to their complying with the
conditions specified in Regulation 6 of Notification FEMA No.120/RB-2004 dated
July 7, 2004, as amended from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to allow investments (or financial commitment) up to the permissible limits on receipt of application in form odi together with form a-2, duly filled in

controls.control_objective: To ensure also comply with the norms stipulated at regulation 7 of notification fema no
controls.control_description:
Investment (or financial commitment)
in financial services should also comply with the norms stipulated at Regulation 7 of
Notification FEMA No.120/RB-2004 dated July 7, 2004, as amended from time to
time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also comply with the norms stipulated at regulation 7 of notification fema no

controls.control_objective: To ensure certify that prior approvals from the regulatory authorities concerned in india and abroad have been obtained
controls.control_description:
While forwarding the report of remittance in respect of investment (or financial
commitment) in financial services sector, AD Category - I banks may certify that
prior approvals from the Regulatory Authorities concerned in India and abroad have
been obtained.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to certify that prior approvals from the regulatory authorities concerned in india and abroad have been obtained

controls.control_objective: To ensure to ensure that the necessary documents, as prescribed in form odi, have been submitted and found to be in order
controls.control_description:
Before allowing the remittance (or financial commitment), AD
Category - I banks are required to ensure that the necessary documents, as
prescribed in form ODI, have been submitted and found to be in order.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to ensure that the necessary documents, as prescribed in form odi, have been submitted and found to be in order

controls.control_objective: To ensure note that an additional timeline of 15 days is made available to them for reporting of investments/ financial commitments by their constituents to rbi
controls.control_description:
Explanation: AD banks may note that an additional timeline of 15 days is made
available to them for reporting of investments/ financial commitments by their
constituents to RBI in the OID application (other than first remittance, which
requires to be reported in ODI system before executing the transaction, to generate
UIN) and is not to be availed by the Indian parties/ Indian Residents for submission
of Forms and documents to the AD bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to note that an additional timeline of 15 days is made available to them for reporting of investments/ financial commitments by their constituents to rbi

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to be submitted by the designated ad category - i bank, in part iii of form odi, to reserve bank in the online application
controls.control_description:
73
dated June 29, 2011, a report should continue to be submitted
by the designated AD Category - I bank, in Part III of form ODI,
to Reserve Bank in the online application.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to be submitted by the designated ad category - i bank, in part iii of form odi, to reserve bank in the online application

controls.control_objective: To ensure be submitted to the reserve bank as per the existing procedure
controls.control_description:
In all other cases of
disinvestment, an application along with the necessary
supporting documents should be submitted to the Reserve
Bank as per the existing procedure.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be submitted to the reserve bank as per the existing procedure

controls.control_objective: To ensure be made and reported online, only after receipt of auto generated e-mail from rbi confirming the uin
controls.control_description:
<image>
However, subsequent remittances (or financial commitment)
under the automatic route and remittances (or financial
commitment) under the approval route should be made and
reported online, only after receipt of auto generated e-mail from
RBI confirming the UIN.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be made and reported online, only after receipt of auto generated e-mail from rbi confirming the uin

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow remittance towards loan to the jv / wos and / or issue guarantee to / on behalf of the jv / wos abroad only
controls.control_description:
(4) AD Category - I banks should allow remittance towards loan to the JV
/ WOS and / or issue guarantee to / on behalf of the JV / WOS abroad only
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to allow remittance towards loan to the jv / wos and / or issue guarantee to / on behalf of the jv / wos abroad only

controls.control_objective: To ensure obtain prior approval from the reserve bank before allowing the remittances towards the loan/issue of guarantee to/on behalf of the overseas jv/wos
controls.control_description:
However, wherever the laws of the host country permit incorporation of a
company without equity participation by the Indian Party, AD banks may
obtain prior approval from the Reserve Bank before allowing the remittances
towards the loan/issue of guarantee to/on behalf of the overseas JV/WOS.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to obtain prior approval from the reserve bank before allowing the remittances towards the loan/issue of guarantee to/on behalf of the overseas jv/wos

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit details of the capitalisation in form odi to the designated branch of the ad category - i bank
controls.control_description:
In such cases also, the Indian Party is required to
submit details of the capitalisation in form ODI to the designated branch of the AD
Category - I bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit details of the capitalisation in form odi to the designated branch of the ad category - i bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure to obtain a custom certified copy of the invoice as required under regulation 12(2) and forward it to the reserve bank together with the revised form
controls.control_description:
Further, in
cases where the export proceeds are being capitalised in accordance with the
provisions of Regulation 11, the AD Category - I banks are required to obtain a
custom certified copy of the invoice as required under Regulation 12(2) and forward
it to the Reserve Bank together with the revised form ODI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to obtain a custom certified copy of the invoice as required under regulation 12(2) and forward it to the reserve bank together with the revised form

controls.control_objective: To ensure make an application in form odi to the reserve bank for consideration
controls.control_description:
Capitalisation of export
proceeds or other entitlements, which are overdue, would require prior approval of
the Reserve Bank for which the Indian Parties should make an application in form
ODI to the Reserve Bank for consideration.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to make an application in form odi to the reserve bank for consideration

controls.control_objective: To ensure to be quoted in all correspondence with the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Allotment of Unique Identification Number (UIN)
The Unique Identification Number allotted to each JV or WOS abroad, is required to
be quoted in all correspondence with the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to be quoted in all correspondence with the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow investment (or financial commitment) in an overseas concern set up by an indian party, in terms of regulation 6 of notification no
controls.control_description:
AD Category - I banks
may allow investment (or financial commitment) in an overseas concern set up by
an Indian Party, in terms of Regulation 6 of Notification No.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to allow investment (or financial commitment) in an overseas concern set up by an indian party, in terms of regulation 6 of notification no

controls.control_objective: To ensure be in accordance with the provisions of notification no
controls.control_description:
AD Category - I bank
may also obtain an undertaking from the applicants to the effect that future sale /
transfer of shares so acquired by Non-Residents in the Indian company shall be in
accordance with the provisions of Notification No.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be in accordance with the provisions of notification no

controls.control_objective: To ensure 3, 2000, as amended from time to time
controls.control_description:
FEMA 20/2000-RB dated May 3,
2000, as amended from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to 3, 2000, as amended from time to time

controls.control_objective: To ensure allow remittances under these specific approvals granted by the reserve bank and report the same to the chief general manager, foreign exchange depart
controls.control_description:
AD Category - I
banks may allow remittances under these specific approvals granted by the
Reserve Bank and report the same to the Chief General Manager, Foreign
Exchange Department, Central Office, Overseas Investment Division, Amar
Building, 5th floor, Mumbai 400 001 in form ODI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to allow remittances under these specific approvals granted by the reserve bank and report the same to the chief general manager, foreign exchange depart

controls.control_objective: To ensure make remittances for purchase of foreign securities in the knowledge based sector under the adr / gdr linked esops, up to the ceiling as stipulated by
controls.control_description:
Purchase of foreign securities under ADR / GDR linked Stock Option
Scheme
AD Category - I banks may make remittances for purchase of foreign securities in
the knowledge based sector under the ADR / GDR linked ESOPs, up to the ceiling
as stipulated by the Reserve Bank from time to time after satisfying that the issuing
company has followed the relevant guidelines of SEBI / Government.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to make remittances for purchase of foreign securities in the knowledge based sector under the adr / gdr linked esops, up to the ceiling as stipulated by

controls.control_objective: To ensure , on being approached by an indian party which is eligible for investment (or
controls.control_description:
(i) In terms of Regulation 14 of the Notification, AD Category - I banks may, on
being approached by an Indian Party which is eligible for investment (or
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , on being approached by an indian party which is eligible for investment (or

controls.control_objective: To ensure issue bid bond guarantee on their behalf for participation in bidding or tender procedure for acquisition of a company incorporated outside india
controls.control_description:
August 22, 2022
financial commitment) under Regulation 6 of the Notification ibid, allow
remittance towards Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) to the extent eligible after
obtaining Form A2 duly filled in or may issue bid bond guarantee on their
behalf for participation in bidding or tender procedure for acquisition of a
company incorporated outside India.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to issue bid bond guarantee on their behalf for participation in bidding or tender procedure for acquisition of a company incorporated outside india

controls.control_objective: To ensure remit the acquisition value after obtaining form a2 duly filled in and report such remittance (including the amount initially remitted towards emd) to
controls.control_description:
On winning the bid, AD banks may
remit the acquisition value after obtaining Form A2 duly filled in and report
such remittance (including the amount initially remitted towards EMD) to the
Chief General Manager, Foreign Exchange Department, Central Office,
Overseas Investment Division, Amar Building, 5th floor, Mumbai 400 001 in
form ODI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to remit the acquisition value after obtaining form a2 duly filled in and report such remittance (including the amount initially remitted towards emd) to

controls.control_objective: To ensure advise the indian party that in case they are not successful in the bid, they should ensure that the amount remitted is repatriated in accordance with
controls.control_description:
AD Category - I banks, while permitting remittance towards EMD
should advise the Indian Party that in case they are not successful in the bid,
they should ensure that the amount remitted is repatriated in accordance
with Foreign Exchange Management (Realisation, repatriation and surrender
of foreign exchange) Regulations, 2015 (cf.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to advise the indian party that in case they are not successful in the bid, they should ensure that the amount remitted is repatriated in accordance with

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit full details of remittance allowed towards emd / invoked bid bond guarantee, to the chief general manager, foreign exchange department, central
controls.control_description:
(ii) In cases where an Indian Party, after being successful in the bid / tender
decides not to proceed further with the investment, AD banks should submit
full details of remittance allowed towards EMD / invoked bid bond guarantee,
to the Chief General Manager, Foreign Exchange Department, Central
Office, Overseas Investment Division, Amar Building, 5th floor, Mumbai 400
001.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit full details of remittance allowed towards emd / invoked bid bond guarantee, to the chief general manager, foreign exchange department, central

controls.control_objective: To ensure obtain approval from the reserve bank by submitting form odi
controls.control_description:
(iii) In case the Indian Party is successful in the bid, but the terms and conditions
of acquisition of a company outside India are not in conformity with the
provisions of Regulations in Part I, or different from those for which approval
under sub-regulation (3) was obtained, the Indian entity should obtain
approval from the Reserve Bank by submitting form ODI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to obtain approval from the reserve bank by submitting form odi

controls.control_objective: To ensure report details of the disinvestment in the online oid application through the ad category - i bank within 30 days of disinvestment in
controls.control_description:
Transfer by way of sale of shares of a JV / WOS outside India
The Indian Party should report details of the disinvestment in the online OID
application through the AD Category - I bank within 30 days of disinvestment in
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to report details of the disinvestment in the online oid application through the ad category - i bank within 30 days of disinvestment in

controls.control_objective: To ensure be repatriated to india immediately on receipt thereof and in
controls.control_description:
Sale proceeds of
shares / securities shall be repatriated to India immediately on receipt thereof and in
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be repatriated to india immediately on receipt thereof and in

controls.control_objective: To ensure , henceforth, be retained by the designated ad category -i bank, who would be required to monitor the receipt of such documents and satisfy themselves
controls.control_description:
Verification of evidence of investment
The share certificates or any other document as evidence of investment, where
share certificates are not issued shall, henceforth, be retained by the designated
AD Category -I bank, who would be required to monitor the receipt of such
documents and satisfy themselves about the bonafides of the documents so
received.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , henceforth, be retained by the designated ad category -i bank, who would be required to monitor the receipt of such documents and satisfy themselves

controls.control_objective: To ensure to be routed through a designated account, an indian party is allowed to open, hold and maintain foreign currency account (fca) abroad for the purpose
controls.control_description:
Opening of Foreign Currency Account abroad by an Indian Party
Wherever, the host country Regulations stipulate that the investments (or financial
commitment) into the country are required to be routed through a designated
account, an Indian Party is allowed to open, hold and maintain Foreign Currency
Account (FCA) abroad for the purpose of overseas direct investments (or financial
commitment) subject to certain terms and conditions stipulated under A.P.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to be routed through a designated account, an indian party is allowed to open, hold and maintain foreign currency account (fca) abroad for the purpose
